source_alignment.subject: Mathematics
source_alignment.source_statement: Recognise and name common 2-D and 3-D shapes; describe position, direction and movement; compare, describe and solve practical problems for lengths and heights, mass or weight, capacity and volume, and time; measure and begin to record these quantities; recognise and know the value of different denominations of coins and notes; and sequence events in chronological order.
source_alignment.derived_objective_note: This review pack integrates Year 1 shape language with direct comparison and practical measuring. It keeps shape identity separate from colour, size and orientation, uses non-standard units before formal recording, and treats money and time through familiar low-reading-load contexts.

objective.child_goal: I can name shapes, describe where things are and compare or measure everyday objects.
objective.statement: Recognise common 2-D and 3-D shapes, describe simple position and movement, and compare or measure length, height, mass, capacity, time and money in practical Year 1 contexts.
misconceptions: Names a shape from its colour, size or orientation instead of its defining features.
Uses 2-D and 3-D shape names as if they mean the same thing, for example calling a cube a square.
Compares length from unaligned starting points or counts gaps instead of equal units.
Assumes a taller or wider container always holds more without comparing capacity.
Assumes the physically larger coin has the greater value.
Confuses the order or duration of familiar events with clock-reading.

vocabulary.child_explanation: A flat shape, such as a circle, triangle, square or rectangle.
vocabulary.audio_script: A two-D shape is flat. Trace around its edge.
vocabulary.visual_anchor: Four flat shape cards with tactile-style outlines.

vocabulary.child_explanation: A solid shape you can hold, such as a cube, cuboid, sphere or cylinder.
vocabulary.audio_script: A three-D shape is solid. It can have flat or curved surfaces.
vocabulary.visual_anchor: A cube, cuboid, sphere and cylinder on a plain tray.

vocabulary.child_explanation: How long something is from one end to the other.
vocabulary.audio_script: Length tells us how long something is. Line up the starting ends.
vocabulary.visual_anchor: Two ribbons sharing one start line.

vocabulary.child_explanation: How much a container can hold.
vocabulary.audio_script: Capacity means how much fits inside a container.
vocabulary.visual_anchor: Two containers being filled with the same small cups.

vocabulary.child_explanation: How much a coin or note is worth.
vocabulary.audio_script: A coin's value is what it is worth, not how big it looks.
vocabulary.visual_anchor: UK coin cards with spoken denomination labels.

vocabulary.child_explanation: Before happens earlier. After happens later.
vocabulary.audio_script: Before is earlier. After is later.
vocabulary.visual_anchor: Three familiar routine pictures on a first-next-last strip.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Look or feel carefully. Tap one feature you notice.
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Activate observation and comparison language without requiring shape naming.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: Notice the edge, corner, surface or end. There is no need to answer quickly.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: One large object or shape with optional outline, corner and surface hotspots.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Sort the flat shapes and solid shapes.
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Establish the distinction between 2-D flat shapes and 3-D solid shapes.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: Flat shapes can be drawn on a card. Solid shapes can be held and turned.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: A flat-shape mat and solid-shape tray with audio-labelled examples.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Which ribbon is longer? Then measure it with equal cubes.
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Model direct comparison, aligned starts and equal non-standard units.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: First line up the ends. Then place equal cubes with no gaps or overlaps.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: Two ribbons share a start line; cubes fill the longer ribbon end to end.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Choose a station: length, mass or capacity. Compare, then tell how you know.
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Practise practical comparison using appropriate models and spoken reasoning.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: Use the tool that matches the question. You may tap, move, point or tell an adult.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: A ruler-free cube strip, balance scale and cup-fill board shown one at a time.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Listen, then choose the picture, coin or movement that matches.
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Check transfer to event order, coin value and positional language without heavy reading.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: Hear the whole prompt. Replay it if you need to. Then choose.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: One of three low-clutter boards: routine sequence, UK coin choices or object-position scene.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Pip left gaps between the cubes. Teach Pip how to measure fairly.
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Consolidate equal-unit iteration and explanation through a repair task.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: I counted six cubes, but I left gaps. Can you fix my measuring and explain why?
teaching_sequence.visual_model: A deliberately gapped cube line beside an empty aligned measuring lane.

manipulatives.purpose: Recognise, sort and describe common shapes and simple position or movement.
manipulatives.interaction_notes: Children tap, single-tap-place, keyboard-step or switch-scan shape cards; spoken feature labels and a turn control support orientation changes.
manipulatives.accessibility_notes: Large targets, thick outlines, colour-independent labels, tactile-print companion option, no timed rotation and static alternatives for movement.

manipulatives.purpose: Compare length, height, mass and capacity with aligned objects, equal units, balance scales and repeated cup fills.
manipulatives.interaction_notes: Auto-place and undo are available; children may choose, point, speak or use partner-assisted scanning instead of dragging.
manipulatives.accessibility_notes: No real lifting, pouring or fine-motor precision is required; count summaries are spoken and visual models do not rely on colour alone.

manipulatives.purpose: Sequence familiar events and recognise UK coin values from produced spoken prompts and clear images.
manipulatives.interaction_notes: Prompt audio plays before choices; each choice has a separate audio label and replay does not reduce evidence.
manipulatives.accessibility_notes: No assumption of shopping experience, analogue-clock mastery or speech; routine contexts are culturally broad and can be replaced by school-day examples.

practice.interleaving_notes: Block 2-D/3-D distinction and direct comparison first. Interleave shape, position, measurement, event order and coin value only after the learner can identify the question type from audio and pictures.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Recognise common 2-D and 3-D shapes across varied colours, sizes and orientations.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: spoken shape or feature prompt + 2-4 shape cards + optional edge, corner or surface evidence
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Include non-prototypical triangles and rotated rectangles; reject distorted drawings and avoid defining a shape by colour.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Follow and describe simple position, direction, whole turns, half turns and quarter turns.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: single uncluttered scene + spoken positional or movement instruction + tap/place/choose response
variant_blueprints.review_notes: State the reference object clearly; offer static before-and-after frames and never require rapid animation tracking.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Compare and begin to measure length, height, mass and capacity using fair practical methods.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: paired objects or containers + align/balance/fill model + compare word or non-standard-unit count
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Units must be equal with no hidden gaps; mass and capacity claims must be demonstrated rather than inferred from appearance.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Sequence familiar events, compare simple durations and recognise UK coin values.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: short produced audio + 2-4 picture or coin choices + separate replayable labels + explain/check prompt
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Use current UK denominations, avoid price knowledge, and separate event order or duration from formal clock reading.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Retain shape features, fair comparison and practical measure language across spaced review.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: review queue weighted to prior misconception + one spoken task-type cue + one representation switch + short teach-back option
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Keep daily review calm, untimed and low reading; oral, pointing and switch responses must carry equivalent evidence.

body.prompt: Which shape is a triangle?
body.supported_interaction: select and place cards using taps, numbered positions or step controls; dragging is optional. Equivalent routes include touch, keyboard, single-switch scanning, eye-gaze dwell selection, AAC or pointing, and adult-scribed responses directed by the learner; no handwriting, speech or fine drag is mandatory.

hints: Do not use colour or which way it points.
A triangle has three straight sides.
question_variants.explanation: The rotated shape is still a triangle because it has three straight sides.

feedback.correct: After the response is checked, the accepted answer is value: "rotated_triangle". The rotated shape is still a triangle because it has three straight sides.
feedback.repair: Do not use colour or which way it points. A triangle has three straight sides. Use that clue to repair appearance over defining features, then try the same decision again.
feedback.evidence: For this shape sort, the number, equation or representation must show the same quantity and relationship. The mathematical evidence supports value: "rotated_triangle": The rotated shape is still a triangle because it has three straight sides.
feedback.misconception_check: appearance over defining features
feedback.support_message: Use the concrete or visual model and respond by touch, keyboard, switch, eye gaze, AAC or pointing, or direct an adult to scribe the chosen number or step. Fine dragging, handwriting and speech are optional. Retry without a timer, loss or penalty.

body.prompt: Line up the ribbons. Which ribbon is longer?
body.supported_interaction: select a labelled choice or enter the response with large, focusable controls. Equivalent routes include touch, keyboard, single-switch scanning, eye-gaze dwell selection, AAC or pointing, and adult-scribed responses directed by the learner; no handwriting, speech or fine drag is mandatory.

hints: Put both ends on the same start line.
Look at which ribbon reaches farther.
question_variants.explanation: With the starting ends aligned, the seven-unit ribbon reaches farther and is longer.

feedback.correct: After the response is checked, the accepted answer is value: "blue_ribbon_7_units". With the starting ends aligned, the seven-unit ribbon reaches farther and is longer.
feedback.repair: Put both ends on the same start line. Look at which ribbon reaches farther. Use that clue to repair unaligned or gapped measure, then try the same decision again.
feedback.evidence: For this measure compare, the number, equation or representation must show the same quantity and relationship. The mathematical evidence supports value: "blue_ribbon_7_units": With the starting ends aligned, the seven-unit ribbon reaches farther and is longer.
feedback.misconception_check: unaligned or gapped measure
feedback.support_message: Use the concrete or visual model and respond by touch, keyboard, switch, eye gaze, AAC or pointing, or direct an adult to scribe the chosen number or step. Fine dragging, handwriting and speech are optional. Retry without a timer, loss or penalty.

body.prompt: Which container holds more small cups?
body.supported_interaction: select a labelled choice or enter the response with large, focusable controls. Equivalent routes include touch, keyboard, single-switch scanning, eye-gaze dwell selection, AAC or pointing, and adult-scribed responses directed by the learner; no handwriting, speech or fine drag is mandatory.

hints: Do not decide from height alone.
Count the same-size cups that fit.
question_variants.explanation: The short wide container holds seven equal cups, so it has greater capacity than the one holding five.

feedback.correct: After the response is checked, the accepted answer is value: "short_wide". The short wide container holds seven equal cups, so it has greater capacity than the one holding five.
feedback.repair: Do not decide from height alone. Count the same-size cups that fit. Use that clue to repair taller means more capacity, then try the same decision again.
feedback.evidence: For this measure compare, the number, equation or representation must show the same quantity and relationship. The mathematical evidence supports value: "short_wide": The short wide container holds seven equal cups, so it has greater capacity than the one holding five.
feedback.misconception_check: taller means more capacity
feedback.support_message: Use the concrete or visual model and respond by touch, keyboard, switch, eye gaze, AAC or pointing, or direct an adult to scribe the chosen number or step. Fine dragging, handwriting and speech are optional. Retry without a timer, loss or penalty.

body.prompt: Listen, then put the three events in order: wash hands, eat lunch, clear the plate.
body.supported_interaction: select and place cards using taps, numbered positions or step controls; dragging is optional. Equivalent routes include touch, keyboard, single-switch scanning, eye-gaze dwell selection, AAC or pointing, and adult-scribed responses directed by the learner; no handwriting, speech or fine drag is mandatory.

hints: What happens before eating?
What happens after lunch is finished?
question_variants.explanation: First we wash our hands, then eat lunch, and finally clear the plate.

feedback.correct: After the response is checked, the accepted answer is value: "wash hands", "eat lunch", "clear the plate". First we wash our hands, then eat lunch, and finally clear the plate.
feedback.repair: What happens before eating? What happens after lunch is finished? Use that clue to repair event order from picture position, then try the same decision again.
feedback.evidence: For this audio sequence, the number, equation or representation must show the same quantity and relationship. The mathematical evidence supports value: "wash hands", "eat lunch", "clear the plate": First we wash our hands, then eat lunch, and finally clear the plate.
feedback.misconception_check: event order from picture position
feedback.support_message: Use the concrete or visual model and respond by touch, keyboard, switch, eye gaze, AAC or pointing, or direct an adult to scribe the chosen number or step. Fine dragging, handwriting and speech are optional. Retry without a timer, loss or penalty.

body.prompt: Tap the coin worth ten pence.
body.supported_interaction: select a labelled choice or enter the response with large, focusable controls. Equivalent routes include touch, keyboard, single-switch scanning, eye-gaze dwell selection, AAC or pointing, and adult-scribed responses directed by the learner; no handwriting, speech or fine drag is mandatory.

hints: Listen to each coin value.
Look for the coin marked ten pence.
question_variants.explanation: The 10p coin is worth ten pence. Coin size does not tell the value by itself.

feedback.correct: After the response is checked, the accepted answer is value: "10p". The 10p coin is worth ten pence. Coin size does not tell the value by itself.
feedback.repair: Listen to each coin value. Look for the coin marked ten pence. Use that clue to repair larger coin greater value, then try the same decision again.
feedback.evidence: For this coin choice, the number, equation or representation must show the same quantity and relationship. The mathematical evidence supports value: "10p": The 10p coin is worth ten pence. Coin size does not tell the value by itself.
feedback.misconception_check: larger coin greater value
feedback.support_message: Use the concrete or visual model and respond by touch, keyboard, switch, eye gaze, AAC or pointing, or direct an adult to scribe the chosen number or step. Fine dragging, handwriting and speech are optional. Retry without a timer, loss or penalty.

hints: Ignore colour, size and which way it points.

feedback.misconception_check: Check the appearance over defining features idea against the task evidence.
feedback.support_message: Touch, keyboard, switch, eye-gaze, AAC, pointing and adult-supported routes are valid; speed, speech and fine-motor precision are not scored.

misconception_repairs.misconception: Names a shape from its colour, size or orientation instead of its defining features.
misconception_repairs.detect_with: The child rejects a rotated or unusually proportioned example but accepts a familiar-looking distractor.
repair_sequence: Trace and count the relevant sides, corners or surfaces.
Sort a fresh set with the same shape shown in three orientations.
misconception_repairs.evidence_of_repair: The child identifies a new non-prototypical shape and names a defining feature.

misconception_repairs.misconception: Uses 2-D and 3-D shape names as if they mean the same thing, for example calling a cube a square.
misconception_repairs.detect_with: The child uses a flat face name for the whole solid or places solids on the flat-shape mat.
repair_sequence: Place a square card beside a cube.
Trace the flat square face, then turn the whole cube.
Sort a new flat/solid pair and say card shape or solid shape.
misconception_repairs.evidence_of_repair: The child distinguishes a square from a cube and a circle from a sphere in a new item.

misconception_repairs.misconception: Compares length from unaligned starting points or counts gaps instead of equal units.
misconception_repairs.detect_with: The child chooses the object placed farther right or reports a unit count containing gaps or overlaps.
repair_sequence: Move both objects to one start line.
Place equal units touching end to end.
Recount the units while checking each covers part of the length.
misconception_repairs.evidence_of_repair: The child aligns and measures a fresh pair accurately with equal units.

misconception_repairs.misconception: Assumes a taller or wider container always holds more without comparing capacity.
misconception_repairs.detect_with: The child predicts from one visible dimension and ignores equal-cup filling evidence.
repair_sequence: Record the prediction without marking it wrong.
Fill both containers with the same small cup.
Compare the counts and explain that capacity is how much fits inside.
misconception_repairs.evidence_of_repair: The child uses repeated equal fills to compare a new container pair.

misconception_repairs.misconception: Assumes the physically larger coin has the greater value.
misconception_repairs.detect_with: The child orders coins by diameter rather than denomination.
repair_sequence: Cover the relative size cue with equal card frames.
Replay each denomination and match it to its numeral label.
Choose the greater value from a fresh pair.
misconception_repairs.evidence_of_repair: The child selects a higher-value smaller coin from a reviewed UK coin pair.

adaptive_support.low_sensory: Use plain mats, quiet confirmation, no background music beneath speech and one comparison at a time.
adaptive_support.reduced_motion: Replace turns, pours, balance movement and object travel with static before-and-after frames and instant state changes.
adaptive_support.audio_first: Speak every prompt, shape name, positional word, measure comparison and coin denomination with replay and visible playback state.
adaptive_support.reading_support: Use objects, shape outlines, symbols and short labels; written instructions never carry the only clue.
adaptive_support.attention_support: Use a predictable notice-compare-check-say routine and hide tools not needed for the current measure.
adaptive_support.processing_support: Allow unlimited replay, auto-alignment, auto-placement, extra wait time and stepwise reveals.
adaptive_support.confidence_support: Preserve correct observations, distinguish motor placement from mathematical judgement and use neutral try-another-way repair.
adaptive_support.eal_support: Preteach flat, solid, side, corner, surface, longer, shorter, heavier, lighter, full, empty, before, after and value with objects and gestures.

evidence.mastery_rule: Expected requires accurate common-shape recognition plus fair comparison in at least two measure contexts and one audio-led time, money or position task. Secure requires transfer across orientation or representation and retained equal-unit reasoning after spaced review.
evidence.parent_summary: Can name common flat and solid shapes, describe simple position, and compare or measure everyday objects using fair methods.
evidence.teacher_evidence: Records shape-feature evidence separately from language, motor route and visual access; records aligned comparison, unit use and practical-measure reasoning by context.
next_actions: Route appearance-based shape choices to feature tracing.
Route unfair measuring to start-line and equal-unit repair.
Revisit mass and capacity with demonstrated comparisons rather than visual guessing.
Schedule mixed retrieval at 1, 3, 7, 14 and 30 days.

qa.notes: Review-stage Year 1 shape-and-measures pack with a deterministic 240-item pilot bank. Five curated variants are preserved alongside candidates covering common 2-D and 3-D shape recognition and properties, varied orientation, referenced position, quarter and half turns, fair direct comparison and equal non-standard units for length, height, mass and capacity, plus event order, duration and time language. Generated candidates include concrete, tactile, visual, verbal and non-drag motor routes; feedback repairs appearance, alignment, unit-gap, capacity-height, size-mass and viewer-reference misconceptions. Joyful builder missions remain untimed and reward observing, comparing and checking rather than speed. Referenced audio is ElevenLabs-gated for human listening review with browser TTS prohibited. Independent mathematics, SEND, coin-image, narration and renderer review remains required before promotion.

accessibility_policy.no_timer_or_penalty: Learners are never timed for mastery and never lose earned progress for mistakes; incorrect attempts route to calm repair, replay and retry.
accessibility_policy.equivalent_response_routes: Every child-facing task must preserve an equivalent route through touch, keyboard, switch, eye-gaze, AAC, pointing, partner-mediated or adult-scribed response where appropriate.
accessibility_policy.audio_equivalent: Audio and read-aloud support must complement visible text, visuals and replay. Child-facing narration must use produced, human-listening-approved assets before release; browser TTS is not a teaching or phonics fallback.
accessibility_policy.reduced_motion: Reduced-motion and static alternatives must preserve the same curriculum evidence, feedback and reward meaning without relying on animation.
